summary: Backend + Frontend end-to-end testing of Riba DAW. 7/7 backend pytest cases pass against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Frontend Playwright covered all 25+ flows in the review request: brand visible, dream-generate dialog → procedural dream track row appears, MIDI track add, per-track play/mute/solo/volume/pan/EQ-toggle/EQ bass-mid-high, piano roll open/close, transport play/stop, metronome toggle, tempo + master volume sliders, save/load, dream history modal, Magic12 stem separation (adds 4 stems over ~3s), Magic12 mastering modal (LLM fallback text), MIDI↔Audio convert, theme toggle (dark↔light), manual modal, keyboard shortcuts (Space, M, F1, 1-9), and Clear All (window.confirm). LLM fell back to procedural/static path as expected (budget exceeded) - response shapes still valid.

issues: Hero background image (piano/lights photo) appears stretched/dim behind the empty-state card; consider a tighter overlay so the central card has stronger contrast. Cosmetic only.

critical_code_review_comments: Daw.jsx is 966 lines - consider splitting transport bar, side panels and modals into smaller components for maintainability (>700 line guideline).

Frontend mocked features (Magic12 stem separation, MIDI↔Audio convert, MP3 export which exports JSON) are clearly flagged in agent_to_agent_context but end users may be misled - consider a small 'simulated' badge on those buttons.
Backend uses @app.on_event('shutdown') which is deprecated in FastAPI; lifespan handlers are recommended but currently harmless.
DreamResponse model returns generated `notes` but procedural fallback doesn't propagate `tempo` into the response, while it is stored in DB. Minor consistency nit.
seed_data_creation: TEST_session_1 created and deleted by test_session_full_crud; multiple dream history entries created via /api/dream/generate (kept in db.dream_history).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_riba_api.py - run with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. The Emergent LLM key budget is exceeded so /api/dream/generate and /api/mastering/suggest use the procedural fallback (still returns valid schema). When running Playwright, use `page.on('dialog', async_handler)` not `page.once('dialog', lambda d: asyncio.create_task(d.accept()))` because that pattern silently fails in Playwright-Python's async API. EQ sliders use range 0-100 (not -12 to +12 dB). Theme toggle changes the inner DAW container background, NOT document.body.
rca of the issue: First Playwright pass had 5 apparent failures that were all test-script artifacts, not real bugs: (1) eq-mid slider .fill('-2') failed because the slider min=0/max=100 - fixed by using values in range; (2) Magic12 added 0 tracks because a previously opened Dream History modal still had focus and intercepted the click - fixed by ordering the actions before opening any blocking modal; (3) theme bg unchanged was because I queried document.body but theme is applied to the inner 100vh div - fixed by querying that div; (4) midi/audio convert appeared to add 0 because I checked tracks count too early relative to the 800ms setTimeout - fixed with longer wait; (5) Clear All left tracks because the dialog handler used `page.once + asyncio.create_task(d.accept())` which does not await - fixed by registering an async dialog handler. Second pass: all features pass.
